understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' in the semantic grid, with the key requirement that if there are multiple '+' tiles they must all be occupied at the same time for the engine to report WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that receives movement commands. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece in that direction by a fixed step size (in the observed run, horizontal moves changed x by 3: [0,21]->[3,21] and [15,21]->[12,21]). CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate; if the destination is reachable/valid (not blocked by walls/obstacles and consistent with the level's movement rules), the piece relocates there (e.g., [0,21]<->[15,21]), otherwise it does not move (e.g., clicks at [35,17], [39,15], [33,15] were blocked or had no effect). Walls/obstacles prevent movement/relocation; pushing behavior was not confirmed from the observed actions (no clear instance where another object was pushed).

orient_history: Arrow moves only shift the selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) and respect wall collisions. Since both pieces are immobile, progress likely requires CLICK to reposition/teleport/place a selectable onto a goal tile (possibly ignoring intervening walls).
Hypothesis: CLICK changes which selectable is active; movement uses MOVE_* but both pieces appear immobile unless another interaction (e.g., selecting the other piece) enables a different behavior.

CLICK selects a selectable piece (or a location to move it if reachable). Arrow moves attempt to shift the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by walls. Since selectable_18_21 is fully trapped, progress requires switching selection to the other piece and navigating it toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a different selectable (control focus changes), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) but can be blocked by walls. Need to pick a selectable that has an unblocked move path toward the goal, then navigate both onto goal area.

Movement keys are currently impossible because the selected piece is walled in. CLICK likely acts on grid-aligned targets; the earlier goal click may have failed because it was not aligned to the 3-pixel cell grid / hit an edge. Hypothesis: clicking a grid-aligned point inside the goal region will move/teleport the selected piece onto it if allowed.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is not the core movement method to reach the goal here; instead, MOVE_* inputs may change the active/selected piece or shift within a hidden corridor even when CLICK fails. I need to probe MOVE behavior directly since CLICK looping made no progress.

CLICK likely switches control between selectable pieces (selection jump can be reported as the selected piece 'moving'). Since the currently selected piece is fully boxed in, we should switch to the other selectable to see if it has an escape route toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable likely switches selection (or otherwise interacts differently than clicking empty/goal space). We may need to control both pieces to place them onto the goal area; since movement is wall-blocked, progress likely requires selecting/moving the other piece.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece to the clicked tile when it is a valid destination, potentially constrained (e.g., must share the same y/row). Previous successful CLICK moves all kept y=21; failed goal clicks had y!=21. So try clicking a point on the goal that also has y=21 to allow relocation onto the goal.

Arrow moves are single-step (3 px = 1 cell) and respect wall collisions; CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinates if that destination is valid (not all clicks work, likely due to collision/line-of-sight/valid-cell constraints). Currently we’re in a fully blocked pocket, so we need a CLICK relocation to escape.

Hypothesis: CLICKing on the other selectable switches control to it (or swaps positions/selection), which may allow movement from a different spot even when the current selected piece is boxed in. To make progress toward the goal, first change selection to the other selectable.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK seems to relocate the selected piece directly, but it can also get trapped in a pocket where normal MOVE actions are blocked. A MOVE action while blocked may either (a) still advance by snapping to another valid cell, or (b) change which selectable is active when the current one cannot move.

CLICK appears to attempt to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked tile (possibly leaving/creating another selectable behind sometimes). If the destination is unreachable/blocked by walls, the piece will not move. Need to get a selectable onto the goal (+) area.

CLICK can relocate the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel position if that destination is valid/reachable (not blocked by walls/invalid region). It may also allow interacting with another selectable if clicked on its occupied position (possibly switching selection or swapping).

Hypothesis: CLICK either (a) moves the currently selected piece to the clicked location if reachable, or (b) selects another selectable if clicked on it. Since the selected piece is fully blocked, switching to the other selectable may open a new move sequence.

CLICK appears to move/switch the selected piece onto the clicked selectable’s position (effectively selecting/teleporting between selectables). Since normal moves are blocked, I should switch to the other selectable and probe if it enables different interactions/moves toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked cell if it’s a valid empty cell (or swaps if it’s another selectable). Direct MOVE is standard 1-cell step but often blocked by walls/collisions. To reach the goal area, I likely need to CLICK-step through intermediate valid cells toward the right side.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): MOVE_* inputs may change which selectable is controlled when movement is blocked in that direction (e.g., MOVE_LEFT could switch selection to the adjacent selectable at [12,21]), rather than always attempting a pure move.

CLICK likely either selects a piece when clicking on it, or moves the currently selected piece to the clicked location if that destination is valid/reachable. Arrow moves are blocked by adjacent walls from the current position, so switching to the other selectable may be necessary to make progress toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts the selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by walls; CLICK attempts to place/teleport the selected piece to the clicked pixel coordinate if it is a valid, non-wall, grid-aligned (multiple of 3) top-left position. The goal requires placing a selectable onto the goal area; earlier click on [35,17] failed likely due to misalignment.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked pixel location if that destination is valid (not blocked). The earlier goal clicks may have failed due to clicking near/at an invalid point; trying a point clearly inside the goal's bounding box and aligned to the 3-pixel grid may succeed.

CLICKing on a selectable likely switches control to that piece (the log reports it as a 'move'), and different selectables may have different available movement/interaction options. Since the current selected piece is completely trapped, we should switch to the other selectable to test mobility/goal placement from there.

Hypothesis: CLICK primarily changes the currently-selected selectable (the engine reports this as the 'selected piece moved' to the clicked selectable’s position). Arrow moves only work when the selected piece is in a navigable space; current selected piece at [15,21] is fully walled-in, so we must switch selection to the other selectable and test mobility from there.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is primarily changing which selectable is currently controlled (or only works on valid/empty landing cells), and the real way to traverse is via MOVE actions; if a piece is fully enclosed, MOVE may still reveal whether walls can be pushed/shifted or whether only the other selectable has mobility.

Hypothesis: CLICK either (a) selects a different selectable when clicking on it, or (b) attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked location (possibly with path/line constraints and collision checks). Since MOVE_* is fully blocked, progress likely requires switching selection or using CLICK to reposition to a different pocket/corridor.

Clicks appear to switch which selectable is currently controlled (selected), rather than moving through walls. MOVE_* only affects the currently selected piece and can be blocked by walls. The selected piece at [15,21] is trapped, so progress requires selecting the other piece at [3,21].

CLICK appears to be the primary way to reposition/switch pieces (prior clicks moved the active piece between discrete snap positions). Since MOVE_* is fully blocked at [3,21], the only plausible progress is to CLICK directly onto the goal region’s anchor position to attempt a snap/teleport onto it.

Working hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts by one cell (3 pixels) but we are trapped. CLICK attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked pixel-cell if that destination is valid (not colliding / allowed). The goal region may require clicking an exact top-left cell coordinate within the + area; some sampled goal-adjacent clicks may have been slightly off/invalid.

CLICK likely switches which selectable is currently selected when clicking on a selectable’s position (prior logs show the 'selected piece moved' behavior consistent with selection switching). MOVEs only move the currently selected piece, but this selected one is fully trapped, so I must switch to the other and see if it has any legal movement.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK can reposition pieces only when the destination is in a reachable/valid empty region; right now the selected piece is enclosed by walls so neither CLICK nor MOVE will relocate it. Non-CLICK moves may still change selection state or reveal a different interaction rule, so I should test a MOVE action to gather new evidence.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to place/spawn/move the selected 3x3 selectable to the exact pixel coordinate, but it only works when the target aligns to the underlying 3-pixel grid and is not a wall. To win, it’s enough that at least one selectable occupies the goal area. Best remaining chance is a grid-aligned click inside the goal semantic cells (top-left of a goal cell).

Movement and clicks relocate the currently selected 3x3 piece in 3-pixel (1-cell) increments, but collisions with walls prevent placement onto/into blocked regions. With 0 steps remaining, no further state changes can be made this level, so the level cannot be completed from this state.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' in the semantic grid, with the key requirement that if there are multiple '+' tiles they must all be occupied at the same time for the engine to report WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that receives movement commands. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece in that direction by a fixed step size (in the observed run, horizontal moves changed x by 3: [0,21]->[3,21] and [15,21]->[12,21]). CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate; if the destination is reachable/valid (not blocked by walls/obstacles and consistent with the level's movement rules), the piece relocates there (e.g., [0,21]<->[15,21]), otherwise it does not move (e.g., clicks at [35,17], [39,15], [33,15] were blocked or had no effect). Walls/obstacles prevent movement/relocation; pushing behavior was not confirmed from the observed actions (no clear instance where another object was pushed).
